question:
Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Bobby can deadlift 300 pounds at 13.  When he is 18 he can deadlift 100 pounds more than _______% of his previous deadlift.  How many pounds did he add per year?
response:
Bobby can deadlift 300 pounds at 13. When he is 18 he can deadlift 100 pounds more than 50% of his previous deadlift. 

I think 50% because half of 300 is 150, and 150 plus 100 is 250, which is what he can deadlift at 18. Then, to find how many pounds he added per year, I divide 100 by 5 years, which is 20 pounds per year.
